$comment: TEMPLATE — the machine-readable half of governance/13-domain-hard-lines.md. ONE entry per hard line: id 'HL-<n>' binds to the '### <n> — …' heading in 13 (checked both ways by --coverage). Entries with other ids (e.g. 'GOV-05-…') are extra checkable rules from other governance docs — allowed, coverage-exempt. One engine (scripts/check-hard-lines.mjs) runs these SAME checks in pre-commit (--staged), CI (--range), and /gate (--all), so the checks cannot drift apart. Fixtures (shouldMatch) are REQUIRED — self-test rejects a pattern you can't prove fires. Greppable line → 'patterns'. Not greppable (DB constraint/trigger/RLS…) → 'mechanical': false + 'enforcedBy' + 'testFile' (must exist). FILL: replace the two illustrative entries below with YOUR lines (/hardline drives the loop), then remove "template": true — until you do, the scan modes warn instead of binding.

hardLines.$comment: Illustration 1 — a GREPPABLE rule (this one comes from governance/05, hence the non-HL id). Patterns match added lines; 'files' scopes to paths where the rule applies; fixtures prove the regex fires.
hardLines.name: No unchecked types

hardLines.$comment: Illustration 2 — a NON-GREPPABLE line (worked example A in 13 §1): the real enforcement is schema + tests. The manifest records WHERE it lives, and self-test verifies the named test file exists — 'only a hope' becomes at least a named, existing test.
hardLines.name: Provenance
hardLines.enforcedBy: NOT-NULL FK join table + links written in the same transaction as content (see governance/13 §1 Enforcement)
